title: ZAnkoku na Tenshi no TE ZE san Jin
display_title: ZAnkoku na Tenshi no TE ZE san Jin
wikitext:
[]: Zankoku Na... *Tsu-chan Mix*             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Fri Oct 07, 2005 8:57 pm I made a mix on Zankoku na Tenshi no TE-ZE and it came out hilarious. Listen to it here: http://www.number3p.com/Curel%20Angels%20Thesis%20-Tsuchan%20Mix-.wav Okay, it's technically not a mix, just an alternated version of the song but enjoy. =p

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 07, 2005 11:09 pm That sounds an awful lot like the Megumi Hayashibara version of Cruel Angel's Thesis played at double speed....

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Sat Oct 08, 2005 6:19 am It is... I already said: Okay, it's technically not a mix, just an alternated version of the song but enjoy.

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Sat Oct 08, 2005 6:44 am Hey, It's pretty cool!! I can never do that!! Main point: It was OK!

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Sat Oct 08, 2005 6:49 am They had a mix with it in AMV Hell 3 or something like that...

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sat Oct 08, 2005 11:22 am XD She sounds like a Chipmunk.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 08, 2005 4:45 pm Yes. Screwing with Eva music is fun. I know. And did you have to post it as a WAV? Was it impossible to make an mp3? Now slowpokes like me will never hear it.

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Sat Oct 08, 2005 6:34 pm Yeah it wouldn't let me. It said I didn't ahve an mp3 enconder but I do because I was able to make mp3s before >.>

[]: ZAnkoku na Tenshi no TE-ZE *san-Jin*             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Mon Oct 10, 2005 10:31 am So... I suscribe to a newsleter that sends me the latest in underground bands. In my latest issue I got this message: "A new underground band in Japan has surfaced called the "Eva no Otakudom" They're only public song thus far is a remake of the song, "Zankoku na Tenshi no Thesis." With their voices singing. As usual, I have attached the MP3 to the newsletter." So I quickly downloaded and then uploaded the song to my server. Now, the three singers are. Kazuki Yasui impersonates Misato. Daisuke Kawa is the one who managed to sort out legal usage of the song. Daisuke Kawa was the one who got rid of the already singing voice and slowed down the beat a tiny bit in some parts because guys sing lower and it's hard and bad sounding if a guy sings too fast. He impersonates Asuka. The final singer is Hikaru Gokoku. He impersonates Rei. And with that said, enjoy the song: http://www.number3p.com/cruelangel.mp3 "Eva no Otakudom" apparently are fans of the anime and are going to attempt to do a couple Eva songs. I'll see if the next newsletter has more about them. Also from what I heard, Gainax has no plans of releasing their songs on their albums. Quite frankly, "Eva no Otakudom" doesn't even have an album themselves. They just make songs for fun it seems.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiromakuta » Mon Oct 10, 2005 3:01 pm Interesting...

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Mon Oct 10, 2005 3:33 pm Holy crap, that was terrible .

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Mon Oct 10, 2005 3:39 pm Space Penis wrote: Holy crap, that was terrible .

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Thu Oct 13, 2005 4:26 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Yes. Screwing with Eva music is fun. I know. And did you have to post it as a WAV? Was it impossible to make an mp3? Now slowpokes like me will never hear it.
